LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT K.J. WOODS CONSTRUCTION, INC. MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT DEMONSTRATES GOOD CAUSE TO VACATE THE DEFAULT UNDER CCP 473 AND FASUYI V. PERMATEX, INC. (2008) 167 CAL.APP.4TH 681. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR FEES AND COSTS ARE DENIED BECAUSE HIS COUNSEL FAILED TO RETURN MS. QUEANO'S PHONE CALL AND THEN OBTAINED THE DEFAULT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. APPEARANCES - STEPHEN V. HARRINGTON OF CLAPP MORENEY, TEL. NO. (650) 989-5400 APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT K.J. WOODS CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S Motion To Transfer Venue to San Mateo is Granted. No opposition filed. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported. = (302/HEK)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Dec-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEPHEN V. HARRINGTON OF CLAPP MORONEY, TEL. NO. (650) 989-5400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - DEFENDANT K.J. WOODS CONSTRUCTION, INC. MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS Granted. Defendant demonstrates good cause to vacate the default under CCP 473 and Fasuyi v. Permatex, Inc. (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 681. Plaintiff's request for fees and costs are denied because his counsel failed to return Ms. Queano's phone call and then obtained the default. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR #7077 =302/HEK
